comment.body:
Good morning,
The weather is pleasantly cool, the sky is overcast, with only 2% chance of precipitation. Meanwhile, a strict “no ship caravans meeting in puddles” regulation is in effect. Feeling that California Dreamin’ vibe.
On another note, the futures market remained strong overnight

comment.body:
Good Morning Alma. Yesterday’s intraday post you commented that nvda call skew increased and smirk flattened, on a day spot price fell (which you remarked is not usual for a day of fear/risk off). Therefore, you said you post ER we can have a good spot for a “short call edge”. 

Did you mean selling call premiums when you say “short call”?  I’ve been reading your commentary from the weekend update and you were describing there that insiders were lowering exposure prior to ER in order to dollar cost average back into nvda. I think this is the trap you say this morning. So I am personally taking your leaning to say a “short-term LONG call option trade” 

Am I interpreting your meaning correctly?
comment.raw_body:
Good Morning Alma. Yesterday’s intraday post you commented that nvda call skew increased and smirk flattened, on a day spot price fell (which you remarked is not usual for a day of fear/risk off). Therefore, you said you post ER we can have a good spot for a “short call edge”. 

Did you mean selling call premiums when you say “short call”?  I’ve been reading your commentary from the weekend update and you were describing there that insiders were lowering exposure prior to ER in order to dollar cost average back into nvda. I think this is the trap you say this morning. So I am personally taking your leaning to say a “short-term LONG call option trade” 

Am I interpreting your meaning correctly?

comment.body: A side comment: Alma, I find a lot of gems in your substack, but I start to be a bit lost (and the UX from Substack doesn't really help) as the comments are a wild mixture between basic questions and expert level, now distributed among several chats. What I am looking here for is the part that is not covered by books (e. g. Volatility Trading / Sinclair, Option Pricing / Natenberg) or simply asking DeepSeek (e. g. any kind of greeks) or Internet (e. g. details of VIX pricing) . I am looking for (and finding) your unique insights into interpreting the market signals, understanding market dynamics and compressing it into visualizations (if you need support in the visualization part with Python/DS.js let me know).
